description: Baseline after fixing autoresearch.sh µs parsing (locale issue). render=433µs, parse=7833µs
Loop variable aliasing: use Ruby locals instead of scope.lookup() for loop item and forloop vars inside for bodies
Inline Hash fast-path for non-special key lookups (skip lookup_prop method call). render -1.4%, parse +17% (larger generated code)
Convert lambdas to module methods (LOOKUP, CALL_FILTER, COMPARE, etc.) — method dispatch faster than lambda.call. render -1.9%, parse -15.6%, allocs -10
Zero-alloc TemplateLexer (cursor-based, returns symbol, offsets as ivars) + perfect hash keywords in ExpressionLexer (byte-level dispatch, no .downcase). parse_allocs -10.4%, parse -6.4%
Lazy tag_name extraction: scan bytes directly for tag name with frozen common-tag lookup, skip full content.split+downcase. parse_allocs -6.6%
Inline output_append String ternary in generated code — regression, larger code hurts parse+render
Skip to_s.downcase in Filters.apply for string names (already lowercase from compiler). render -2.0%, render_allocs -136
Lazy seen={} hash in Utils.to_s — only allocate when Hash/Array recursion detection needed. render -6.0%, allocs -314 (!)
Also lazy seen={} in Utils.inspect. Marginal improvement.
Skip assign_local in for loops when body has no partials/nested loops. Saves 2 method calls + 2 hash inserts per iteration.
Scope#stringify_keys: skip key conversion when all keys already strings (just dup). Saves string allocs in hot render path.
Skip ForloopDrop alloc + each_with_index when body doesn't reference forloop. Use plain each, skip index tracking, scope save/restore. render -6.6%, parse -5.8%
